zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary II, Countess of Menteith於大約1295年出生在蘇格蘭培爾時，她的父親Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith27歲，母親Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith16歲。她於大約1320年與John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1360年4月29日在她的家鄉享年66歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Douglas Castle, Douglas, Lanarkshire, Sc: 英国苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素
zh-hans.Blackford, Perthshire, Scotland: 英国苏格兰培爾布拉可福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary II, Countess of Menteith于大约1295年出生在苏格兰培爾时，她的父亲Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith27岁，母亲Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith16岁。她于大约1320年与John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1360年4月29日在她的家乡享年66岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Menteith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the earldom and province of Menteith in Perthshire, named in Gaelic as ‘hill pasture (above) the Teith’, from monadh ‘hill pasture’ + Teith, a river name of obscure origin. This name was introduced to Ulster at the time of the Plantation in the 17th century, where it is now quite numerous in certain areas, particularly Tyrone.

variants.name: Montooth
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Monteith  .

variants.name: Mantooth
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish Monteith orMantach, a nickname from Gaelic manntach ‘stammering’(also ‘toothless’ in Irish), of which one early Welsh recorded form isMantath.

variants.name: Montieth
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Monteith   This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: II,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary II, Countess of Menteith was born about 1295, in Perthshire, Scotland, her father, Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith, was 27 and her mother, Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith, was 16. She married John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith about 1320. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 29 April 1360, in her hometown, at the age of 66, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary II, Countess of Menteith est née environ 1295, à Perthshire, Écosse, son père, Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith environ 1320. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 avril 1360, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary II, Countess of Menteith um 1295 in Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith 27 und ihre Mutter Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith 16. Sie hat um 1320 John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. April 1360 im Alter von 66 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Scotland begraben.

Lei sposato John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith circa 1320. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 29 aprile 1360, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary II, Countess of Menteithが約1295年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアで生まれた時，父Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteithは27歳，母Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteithは16歳であった。約1320年にJohn Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1360年4月29日に故郷で66歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary II, Countess of Menteith(이)가 대략 1295년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1320년 John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1360년 4월 29일 그녀의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary II, Countess of Menteith nasceu aproximadamente 1295, em Perthshire, Scotland, seu pai, Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith aproximadamente 1320. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de abril de 1360, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary II, Countess of Menteith родилась около 1295 г. в местности пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alan Menteith 3rd Earl of Menteith было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Marjory Murdock, of Monteith, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Graham of Dundaff, 9th Earl of Menteith около 1320 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 29 апреля 1360 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.
